---
title: "Final_Project"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
# Generate sample_info.csv
samples <- c(
  "C_0002","C_0003","C_0004","C_0005","C_0006","C_0008","C_0009","C_0010",
  "C_0011","C_0012","C_0013","C_0014","C_0015","C_0016","C_0017","C_0018",
  "C_0019","C_0020","C_0021","C_0022","C_0023","C_0024","C_0025","C_0026",
  "C_0029","C_0031","C_0032","C_0033","C_0035","C_0036","C_0037","C_0038",
  "C_0039","C_0050","C_0053","C_0060","C_0061","C_0062","C_0065","C_0069",
  "C_0070","C_0071","C_0075","C_0076","C_0077","C_0081","C_0082","C_0083",
  "C_0087","H_0001","H_0002","H_0003","H_0005","H_0006","H_0007","H_0008",
  "H_0009","H_0010","H_0012","H_0013","H_0539","H_0657","H_0658","H_0681",
  "H_0695","H_0700","H_0726","H_0740","H_0750"
)

sample_info <- data.frame(
  sample = samples,
  condition = ifelse(startsWith(samples, "C_"), "Control", "Huntington's")
)

write.csv(sample_info, "data/sample_info.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# make_sample_info.R
# Generates sample_info.csv for GSE64810 (Huntington's Disease vs Control)
# Run this once before launching the Shiny app

sample_info <- data.frame(
  sample = c(
    "C_0002","C_0003","C_0004","C_0005","C_0006","C_0008","C_0009","C_0010",
    "C_0011","C_0012","C_0013","C_0014","C_0015","C_0016","C_0017","C_0018",
    "C_0019","C_0020","C_0021","C_0022","C_0023","C_0024","C_0025","C_0026",
    "C_0029","C_0031","C_0032","C_0033","C_0035","C_0036","C_0037","C_0038",
    "C_0039","C_0050","C_0053","C_0060","C_0061","C_0062","C_0065","C_0069",
    "C_0070","C_0071","C_0075","C_0076","C_0077","C_0081","C_0082","C_0083",
    "C_0087",
    "H_0001","H_0002","H_0003","H_0005","H_0006","H_0007","H_0008","H_0009",
    "H_0010","H_0012","H_0013","H_0539","H_0657","H_0658","H_0681","H_0695",
    "H_0700","H_0726","H_0740","H_0750"
  ),
  diagnosis = c(
    rep("Neurologically normal", 49),
    rep("Huntington's Disease", 20)
  ),
  pmi = c(
    2, 2, 2, 2, 5, 2, 3, 2, 2, 19, 15, 21, 10, 20, 21, 17, 19, 24, 26, 17,
    18, 26, 25, 11, 13, 24, 24, 15, 21, 17, 28, 20, 15, 2, 2, 2, 3, 2, 2,
    24, 19, 21, 23, 30, 21, 26, 18, 32, 19,
    37.25, 5.75, 20.5, 19.15, NA, 8, 21.3, 3.73, 6.16, 12.75, 25.1, 14.5,
    24.3, 11, 19.06, 16.15, 15.66, 14.75, 13.58, 16.16
  ),
  age_of_death = c(
    73, 91, 82, 97, 86, 91, 81, 79, 63, 66, 69, 79, 61, 58, 70, 66, 73, 60,
    76, 61, 62, 69, 61, 88, 93, 53, 57, 43, 46, 40, 44, 57, 80, 74, 69, 76,
    78, 87, 86, 54, 68, 106, 52, 46, 36, 55, 57, 66, 64,
    55, 69, 71, 48, 40, 72, 43, 68, 59, 68, 57, 54, 61, 48, 69, 55, 50, 50,
    75, 53
  ),
  rin = c(
    7.7, 7.9, 8.6, 9.1, 8.6, 8.7, 6.0, 8.4, 6.5, 7.1, 7.8, 8.0, 8.2, 8.4,
    8.2, 8.5, 7.5, 7.9, 7.3, 7.8, 6.6, 8.7, 8.1, 7.1, 6.4, 7.3, 8.3, 7.5,
    7.6, 7.5, 8.3, 7.7, 7.3, 8.5, 8.4, 7.5, 7.6, 8.7, 8.7, 8.3, 6.3, 7.6,
    7.4, 8.2, 8.5, 7.6, 7.8, 8.4, 8.7,
    7.1, 7.5, 7.0, 6.9, 6.2, 8.5, 7.4, 7.8, 8.3, 6.0, 6.1, 6.5, 8.1, 7.8,
    7.0, 7.9, 8.0, 9.2, 6.4, 6.0
  ),
  mrna_seq_reads = c(
    120108434, 38420004, 75850406, 150661916, 63607838, 66131458, 69284092,
    60542776, 93702684, 118327116, 89478160, 65377604, 123746070, 67758208,
    72238818, 64688322, 108683922, 83696384, 79487172, 73133936, 94493436,
    62989822, 55810684, 72581752, 59386108, 73283170, 70994352, 69505712,
    62300754, 63961372, 60288132, 61019098, 74892650, 85310070, 167044880,
    103952680, 95393100, 83773400, 115714502, 128459102, 145087692, 86840836,
    99946984, 85890116, 80103722, 82917984, 123118398, 80696360, 77198978,
    74635390, 71015288, 77385918, 82366794, 77123676, 63294390, 71056116,
    66169262, 65341820, 83110358, 71320688, 124222130, 136764622, 85591704,
    78493314, 86412654, 78329378, 86025890, 101997434, 122909122
  ),
  # HD-only fields (NA for controls)
  age_of_onset = c(
    rep(NA, 49),
    44, 63, 52, 25, 34, 55, 28, 45, 35, 52, 40, 42, 36, 42, 50, 36, 33, 27, 60, 38
  ),
  duration = c(
    rep(NA, 49),
    11, 6, 19, 23, 6, 17, 15, 23, 24, 16, 17, 12, 25, 6, 19, 19, 17, 23, 15, 15
  ),
  cag = c(
    rep(NA, 49),
    45, 41, 43, 48, 51, 41, 49, 42, 46, 42, 49, 45, 45, 44, 42, 45, 47, 48, 42, 48
  ),
  vonsattel_grade = c(
    rep(NA, 49),
    3, 3, 3, 4, 4, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 4, 3, 3, 4, 3, 4, 3, 4
  ),
  hv_striatal_score = c(
    rep(NA, 49),
    2.661, 2.644, 2.428, 3.820, 3.522, 2.593, 2.701, 2.668, 2.621, 2.661,
    2.911, 2.132, 3.290, 2.410, 2.484, 3.581, 2.741, 3.598, 2.621, 3.260
  ),
  hv_cortical_score = c(
    rep(NA, 49),
    0.922, 1.081, 1.707, 1.939, 1.431, 0.849, 1.701, 1.701, 1.200, 1.077,
    1.491, 0.401, 1.604, 0.978, 1.088, 2.062, 1.202, 1.201, 2.361, 1.010
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Save to data folder (create it if it doesn't exist)
if (!dir.exists("data")) dir.create("data")
write.csv(sample_info, "data/sample_info.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("Done! sample_info.csv written to data/sample_info.csv\n")
cat("Rows:", nrow(sample_info), "\n")
cat("Controls:", sum(sample_info$diagnosis == "Neurologically normal"), "\n")
cat("HD samples:", sum(sample_info$diagnosis == "Huntington's Disease"), "\n")
```

```{r}
# run_fgsea.R
# Run this ONCE before launching the Shiny app.
# It reads the DE results, ranks genes, runs fgsea against MSigDB Hallmark
# gene sets, and saves the results to data/fgsea_results.csv

# Install packages if you don't have them:
# install.packages("BiocManager")
# BiocManager::install("fgsea")
# install.packages("msigdbr")

library(fgsea)
library(msigdbr)
library(dplyr)

# ── 1. Load DE results ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
de <- read.table(
  "data/GSE64810_mlhd_DESeq2_diffexp_DESeq2_outlier_trimmed_adjust.txt",
  header = TRUE,
  sep = "\t",
  row.names = 1
)

head(de)  # check it loaded correctly

# ── 2. Build a named ranking vector ───────────────────────────────────────────
# We rank by log2FoldChange (positive = up in HD, negative = down in HD).
# Drop rows with NA symbol or NA log2FoldChange
de_clean <- de %>%
  filter(!is.na(symbol), !is.na(log2FoldChange)) %>%
  # If duplicate gene symbols exist, keep the one with highest abs(log2FC)
  group_by(symbol) %>%
  slice_max(abs(log2FoldChange), n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

# Create named numeric vector: names = gene symbols, values = log2FoldChange
ranks <- setNames(de_clean$log2FoldChange, de_clean$symbol)

# Sort descending (required by fgsea)
ranks <- sort(ranks, decreasing = TRUE)

cat("Number of genes ranked:", length(ranks), "\n")

# ── 3. Load Hallmark gene sets for Homo sapiens from MSigDB ──────────────────
# Hallmark = 50 well-defined biological pathways, good starting point
msigdb_hallmark <- msigdbr(species = "Homo sapiens", category = "H")

# Convert to list format required by fgsea
pathways <- split(msigdb_hallmark$gene_symbol, msigdb_hallmark$gs_name)

cat("Number of Hallmark pathways:", length(pathways), "\n")

# ── 4. Run fgsea ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
set.seed(42)  # for reproducibility

fgsea_res <- fgsea(
  pathways = pathways,
  stats    = ranks,
  minSize  = 15,   # minimum gene set size
  maxSize  = 500   # maximum gene set size
)

# ── 5. Clean up and save ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Convert the 'leadingEdge' list column to a string so it can be saved as CSV
fgsea_res$leadingEdge <- sapply(fgsea_res$leadingEdge, paste, collapse = ",")

# Sort by adjusted p-value
fgsea_res <- fgsea_res %>% arrange(padj)

# Save to data folder
if (!dir.exists("data")) dir.create("data")
write.csv(fgsea_res, "data/fgsea_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("Done! fgsea_results.csv written to data/fgsea_results.csv\n")
cat("Significant pathways (padj < 0.05):",
    sum(fgsea_res$padj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

# Quick preview of top results
head(fgsea_res[, c("pathway", "NES", "padj")], 10)
```
